summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that when remote_lookups is enabled, unfurl makes HTTP requests to arbitrary URLs including internal network resources. The PoC demonstrates SSRF by accessing a simulated cloud metadata endpoint and retrieving sensitive data that should not be accessible from external networks.
expected_security_impact: The unfurl application should make HTTP requests to the attacker-controlled URL and return the response content, demonstrating that it can be used as a proxy to access internal network resources, cloud metadata endpoints, or arbitrary external services.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built and launched the unfurl application with remote_lookups enabled, sent a crafted request to access the internal metadata simulator at http://metadata-server/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/, and the unfurl response included the simulated IAM credentials from the internal service, proving the SSRF vulnerability allows unauthorized access to internal resources.
verification_method: The reproduction script sets up a mock metadata server on an internal Docker network, starts the vulnerable unfurl instance with remote_lookups enabled, sends a crafted SSRF payload to access the internal metadata endpoint, and checks if the response contains the expected proof-of-exploitation marker (simulated IAM credentials). The verification confirms that unfurl successfully acted as an SSRF proxy to reach the internal-only service.
evidence_produced_by_run: The unfurl application made HTTP requests to the internal metadata server URL provided in the SSRF payload
The JSON response from unfurl contained the simulated IAM credentials from the metadata endpoint, proving internal resource access
The unfurl logs show the parser chain executing and making remote HTTP requests to the attacker-controlled destination
The metadata server logs confirm it received the request from the unfurl container, demonstrating successful SSRF
The response time and network behavior confirmed that unfurl acted as a proxy without proper URL validation or private IP range blocking

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
